name: Urban League Of Metropolitan Saint Louis Inc - Ferguson Community Empowerment Center
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor serving St. Louis metro with free pre/post-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention, and rental assistance programs.
description_long:
The Urban League of Metropolitan Saint Louis Inc operates the Ferguson Community Empowerment Center as part of a comprehensive community development organization serving the St. Louis region. The Housing Empowerment program is their dedicated division providing HUD-certified housing counseling and financial education to residents facing housing instability or seeking homeownership guidance.

The organization offers a broad suite of housing-related services including individual and group financial counseling, HUD-certified pre- and post-purchase homebuyer education, loss mitigation counseling for mortgage defaults, rental and utility assistance through multiple grant programs, fair housing education, home repair services for low-income elderly and disabled homeowners, and foreclosure prevention support. They operate the Framework course for homeownership education and maintain partnerships with major lenders including Bank of America, Enterprise Bank & Trust, Montgomery Bank, and Wells Fargo.

What distinguishes this organization is its deep community integration and institutional partnerships. The Urban League hosts the annual St. Louis Metropolitan Housing Task Force bringing together lending institutions, government agencies, and nonprofits. They serve on CIBC's Community Advisory Council advising $3 billion in housing investments across four metropolitan areas and have received grants from multiple major banks to fund financial education. They also partner with the Metropolitan St. Louis Equal Housing and Opportunity Council to provide free fair housing law education.

This is a legitimate, well-established nonprofit with documented institutional relationships and HUD certification. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are limited to the St. Louis metropolitan region. While they offer rental assistance, the program relies on specific grant funding (AHTF, ESG, EFSP) which may have availability constraints. Their home repair program is specifically limited to minor repairs for elderly/disabled homeowners at or below 80% area median income.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling program providing expert guidance on homebuying, foreclosure prevention, and fair housing
Free financial literacy classes and money management counseling through partnerships with major banks (Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Enterprise)
Multiple rental and mortgage assistance programs funded through St. Louis City AHTF, County ESG, and FEMA's EFSP to prevent homelessness and eviction
Hosts annual St. Louis Metropolitan Housing Task Force convening lenders, government agencies, and community organizations for systemic coordination
Home repair program specifically serving low-income elderly and disabled homeowners with minor electrical, plumbing, accessibility, and structural repairs
Serves as advisor to CIBC's $3 billion housing investment initiative across four metropolitan areas
Partnership with Metropolitan St. Louis Equal Housing and Opportunity Council for free fair housing law education
cons: Services limited to St. Louis metropolitan region only—not available for consumers outside this geographic area
Rental and mortgage assistance programs depend on specific grant funding (AHTF, ESG, EFSP) which may have availability constraints and income eligibility limits
Home repair program restricted to elderly and/or disabled homeowners at or below 80% area median income, excluding working-age adults
Website does not specify counseling availability hours, wait times, or response times for assistance requests
No clear information on whether services are available in-person, virtual, or both modalities
best_for: First-time homebuyers in St. Louis metro seeking free HUD-certified pre-purchase counseling and financial education
Current homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage default seeking loss mitigation counseling and financial alternatives
Renters at risk of eviction seeking rental assistance through grant-funded programs
Low-income elderly or disabled homeowners needing minor home repairs to maintain housing stability
diagnosis: Best for St. Louis metro residents seeking free, expert HUD-certified housing counseling for homeownership, foreclosure prevention, or eviction assistance. Primary caveat: services are geographically limited to the St. Louis metropolitan region and some assistance programs depend on grant availability and income eligibility thresholds.
services: HUD-certified housing counseling for pre- and post-purchase homebuying
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Eviction prevention through rental and mortgage assistance programs
Financial literacy and money management counseling
Fair housing education and discrimination advocacy
Framework course for smart homeownership education
Home repair services for elderly and disabled homeowners
Utility and rent assistance through AHTF, ESG, and EFSP grants
Group and individual financial counseling sessions
Community partner coordination for housing solutions
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 9420 W Florissant Ave, Saint Louis, MO 63136
meta_description: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor serving St. Louis metro with free pre/post-purchase counseling and foreclosure prevention.
